title: endiandra aggregata
intro: Endiandra aggregata, also known as the clustered walnut, is a species of tree native to Australia and New Guinea. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Endiandra aggregata include infusions and decoctions, which can be made from the bark, leaves, and seeds, although more research is needed to confirm the safety and efficacy of these preparations.
